title: Yen’s Slump Extends Beyond Dollar and Stokes Inflation Fears

title: Stocks Hit by AI and War Jitters as Oil Tops $100: Markets Wrap
summary: A surge in oil spurred by the escalating Iran war sent stocks and bonds lower, with Wall Street also rattled by renewed concerns over whether massive artificial-intelligence investments will pay off.

title: China Tankers Pass Red Sea Chokepoint Despite Houthi Threat
summary: Two China-owned oil tankers loaded with Saudi Arabian crude exited the Red Sea via the Bab el-Mandeb chokepoint despite overnight Houthi attacks on vessels in the region.

title: Miami to Sell Griffin a Historic Site Next to New Citadel HQ
summary: Miami agreed to sell Ken Griffin a site across the street from the planned Citadel headquarters in the Brickell financial district that currently houses an historic cottage, completing the billionaire’s goal of owning the entire square block.

title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 23
summary: Oil surged past $100 a barrel for the first time in two months after Iran-backed Houthi militants said they attacked two Saudi Arabian tankers in the Red Sea, opening a new front in the Middle East conflict and threatening deeper supply disruptions.

title: Tesla Short Sellers Mint $4 Billion Profit as Shares Nosedive
summary: The deep plunge in Tesla Inc.’s stock on Thursday is turning into a major payday for its short sellers.

title: US Treasury Yields Rise to 2026 Highs With Oil Sparking Fed Bets
summary: US Treasury yields rose to their highest levels of the year as the threat of escalation in the Iran war sent oil prices higher and boosted bets that the Federal Reserve could raise interest rates as soon as next week.

title: S&P 500 Heads Toward Worst Day in a Month as Oil Soars Over $100
summary: US stocks tumbled, putting the S&P 500 Index on track for its worst day in a month, as doubts about the payoffs from the debt-fueled artificial-intelligence investment boom dragged down big tech stocks.

title: Heat Worries US Wheat Farmers Just as Crop Prices Are Surging
summary: For farmers in North Dakota surveying their wheat as harvest closes in, scorching heat is threatening yields just as crop prices rise to a three-year high.

title: Magnificent 7 Lose $767 Billion as AI Skeptics Dump Tech Stocks
summary: The Magnificent Seven group of megacap technology stocks is on track for its biggest one-day drop since the tariff tantrum in April 2025 as results from Alphabet Inc. and Tesla Inc. are casting doubt on the durability of the artificial intelligence trade that has powered the stock market for more than three years.

title: Kalshi Eyes Copper Perpetual Futures After Precious Metals Push
BlackRock’s GIP Eyes Bundling Fund Stakes in Securitization Deal
summary: BlackRock Inc.’s Global Infrastructure Partners LP has been in discussions with investors to sell bonds backed by stakes in some of its private capital funds, joining a growing list of asset managers exploring ways to bolster returns on their holdings.

title: NYC’s Former HSBC Tower Seeks More Than $800 Million in Sale
summary: A Manhattan office tower where Amazon.com Inc. has offices is going up for sale.

title: Bund Yields Hit 15-Year High as $100 Oil Fans Inflation Concerns
summary: German bond yields rose to the highest level since 2011 as surging oil prices prompted bigger bets on interest-rate hikes, even as the European Central Bank kept its benchmark unchanged.

title: Chocolate Maker Lindt Faces US Lawsuit Over Child Labor Allegations
summary: Lindt & Spruengli AG has been sued by an activist law firm in the US which alleges the Swiss chocolate maker is falsely promoting its efforts to battle child labor in Ghana and the Ivory Coast from where it sources cocoa.

title: How Leveraged Exchange-Traded Funds Are Suddenly Everywhere
summary: On this episode of Trillions, we discuss what’s fueling the boom and how these exchange-traded funds work in today’s market.

title: Russia’s Black Sea Waters Not Safe for Ships Amid Attacks
summary: Moscow warned that it is unsafe to navigate in its Black Sea waters after Ukraine has been ramping up attacks on Russia-linked ships in the area.

title: The Worrying Signal Embedded in the Momentum Selloff
summary: A loss of market leadership.

title: Ireland Says Alumina Probe Finds No Proof of Russia Weapons Link
summary: An investigation into alumina exports from Ireland to Russia has not found conclusive evidence linking a production plant to the manufacture of weapons in Russia, Irish Prime Minister Micheal Martin said.

title: Gold Falls as Middle East Conflict Reverses Dip-Buying Momentum
summary: Gold fell as escalating conflict in the Middle East continued to push up energy prices, fueling bets the Federal Reserve will tighten monetary policy to contain inflation.
